        byteCount

      • byteCount[T: SomeNumber | enum | bool | char | set](format: static GrimeFormat; x: T): int
Source   Edit  

implements dumping behavior for basic types

Imports

common, dict

Consts

grimeJsFloatUseDataView {.booldefine.} = true
Source   Edit

Source   Edit   
proc dump[T: distinct](format: static GrimeDumpFormat; dumper: var GrimeDumper;
                       v: T) {.inline.}
Source   Edit   
proc dump[T: enum](format: static GrimeDumpFormat; dumper: var GrimeDumper; v: T) {.
    inline.}
Source   Edit   
proc dump[T: ref | ptr](format: static GrimeDumpFormat; dumper: var GrimeDumper;
                        v: T) {.inline.}

on flat mode, handled as an "optional" type, first byte is 1 if exists and 0 if nil on dict mode, data is saved to the dictionary and the final value is the dictionary index

warning: no reference semantics on flat mode means this is prone to infinite recursion if there is a cycle

also warning: VM does not have a way to get reference identity, and JS needs a global map to track it which can be disabled with -d:grimeTrackJsDictReferences=false, so dict mode cannot deal with cycles in those either

Source   Edit

                             dumper: var GrimeDumper; v: T)
